---
title: "Home"
page-layout: full
---

# Nicholas Gladwell

<img src="profile_photo.jpg" width="200" style="display:block;" loading="eager">

I am a pharmacology graduate student with interests in computational analysis, neuropharmacology, and pharmacokinetics/pharmacodynamics, with a focus on understanding drug action across molecular and physiological systems.

---

## Bio
I graduated from Brigham Young University in 2024 with a double major in Exercise Science and Spanish. Relevant coursework included neuroanatomy, physiology, chemistry, and microbiology. I am currently pursuing a master's degree in pharmacology at the University of the Basque Country in Bilbao, Spain.

My current research focuses on neuropharmacology and depression-related therapies, combining experimental techniques such as immunohistochemistry with computational approaches for data analysis and interpretation.

---

## Focus Areas
+ Neuropharmacology and CNS drug action
+ Pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics (including predictive modeling)
+ Computational analysis and data interpretation
+ Receptor pharmacology and ligand binding


---

## Skills and Tools
 + <b>Molecular visualization:</b> PyMOL
 + <b>Data analysis and modeling:</b> Python, Phoenix WinNonlin, GraphPad Prism
 + <b>Image analysis:</b> FIJI/ImageJ
 + <b>Laboratory techniques:</b> Cell culture, PCR, Western blot, immunohistochemistry, confocal microscopy

---

## Projects

### Structural Basis of Functional Selectivity in 5-HT<sub>2A</sub> and 5-HT<sub>2B</sub> Receptors

Comparative analysis of serotonin receptor subtypes using structural biology and computational visualization to assess ligand binding and receptor activation.

[View Project →](serotonin-receptor-analysis.qmd)

### Monte Carlo Simulation of Antibiotic PK Variability and Probability of Target Attainment

Simulating interindividual pharmacokinetic variability to evaluate the probability of achieving therapeutic antibiotic exposure targets.

[View Project →](pk-monte-carlo-simulation.qmd)
